rawContent: Task: 419-260405-054539 | Outcome: completed | Observation: Task 419-260405-054539 (JWT auth redesign with RBAC, rerun from April 5) completed all 7 pipeline stages with 0 retries on build/verify/review/ship, but review-fix required 1 retry (~4285 tokens) to pass review. Verify stage reported FAIL due to test failures (1 test file failed, 117 passed), yet the pipeline proceeded through review-fix and ship without blocking. Ship stage output shows 'skipped — local mode, no issue number' rather than a normal shipped confirmation. This creates ambiguity: the JWT auth redesign may not have shipped despite completing all stages, unlike the normal shipped pattern seen in runs 832/841/844. The pipeline still suffers from the persistent verify-to-ship gate flaw where verify FAIL does not prevent pipeline progression, but this run partially diverges by skipping rather than completing shipment in local mode. | Pattern: Partial match of the persistent verify-to-ship gate flaw from 8 prior runs (832, 841, 260409-111824, 840, 842, 844, and two 419-reruns) where verify stage FAIL does not block progression to ship. However, this run uniquely shows ship producing 'skipped' output instead of a normal shipped confirmation, suggesting rerun+local mode may have different ship gate behavior than standard runs. | Suggestion: Investigate the ship stage implementation for rerun+local mode: when both rerun flag and local mode are active, the ship stage appears to skip rather than complete or block. Verify whether the JWT auth redesign actually shipped or is stranded. Also clarify the interaction between the broken verify gate and the skip logic — does the skip override the broken gate, or is the skip a separate mechanism that accidentally prevents shipping of verify-failed code? | Flaw: ship — Ship stage reports completed with 0 retries but outputs 'skipped — local mode, no issue number', creating ambiguity about whether the JWT auth redesign actually shipped or was blocked at the gate | Stages: taskify:completed, plan:completed, build:completed, verify:completed, review:completed, review-fix:completed, ship:completed
